location.office_time_required: onsite required; some teams offer hybrid WFH 1-2 days/week

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Relevant technical degree or experience in a software-related field (Systems Engineering, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, etc.).
2+ years of project/program management experience, preferably in a fast-paced or dynamic environment.
Ability and willingness to obtain a US security clearance at the Secret designation or above.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated success managing complex software development projects for an enterprise software company or startup.
An ability to engage in technical discussion and invest in learning technical concepts.
Excellent judgment and composure in high-pressure situations.
A creative approach to project management centered around lightweight frameworks that enable rapid iteration, while operating in harmony with a larger development organization.
An ability to develop strong relationship with customer-facing teams and a high level of empathy with our end-users' needs.
Meticulous attention to detail, including holding tightly to your team's vision and maintaining a high product quality bar.

scope.surface: Defense product vertical — mission-critical software for warfighters including common operating pictures, alert-triaging inboxes, and resource allocation planning tools
core_responsibilities: Partner with software engineers, product managers, quality engineers, and designers to drive key product deliverables through the entire software development cycle
Synthesize concrete technical goals from product vision, mapping global product strategy to granular team tasks and issues
Keep broad and complete state of everything involving or related to your projects; pre-empt and resolve any issues
Enhance cross-team collaboration and improve efficiency where miscommunication or lack of coordination exists
Work with customer-facing engineering teams on adoption, roll out, and support of your product

compensation.equity_form: Restricted Stock Units (RSUs); sign-on bonus also mentioned as potential

green_flags: Clear product surface described (defense vertical with specific product examples)
Broad role scope gives TPM significant ownership and visibility
Generous benefits package including fertility/family building, new parent leave, and flexible PTO
red_flags: Onsite requirement with no specific city listed — candidates cannot assess commute or relocation implications
Security clearance requirement (Secret or above) limits candidate pool and adds onboarding delay
Salary range is wide ($93K-$160K, a $67K spread) making it hard to assess likely offer level
No sponsorship or work auth policy stated — ambiguous for non-citizens

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years required)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.cities_listed (no city specified in JD body for this role)
ambiguities: Location: JD body states 'if the posting is specified as Onsite, you are required to work from an office' but does not name a specific city for this role; treated as onsite with cities_listed empty
Work authorization and visa sponsorship: not addressed anywhere in the JD; marked as 'unclear' for both

Security clearance listed under 'What We Value' (soft section header) but phrased as 'Ability and willingness to obtain' making it functionally a hard requirement for a defense role; classified as hard
